name: Cranberry-And-Apple Cobbler
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the flour and corn oil.
2. Add margarine and mix until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. Sprinkle water evenly over the surface of the mixture; stir with a fork until the dry ingredients are moistened.
4. Shape into a ball and gently press between two sheets of heavy-duty plastic wrap into a 4-inch circle.
5. Roll dough into a rectangle that fits a 12x8-inch baking dish.
6. Freeze for 5 minutes, then remove the top layer of plastic wrap.
7. Cut the dough strips to fit the baking dish.
8. In a large bowl, combine fruit (apples and cranberries), brown sugar, tapioca, water, and vanilla extract until well coated.
9. Spoon the fruit mixture into the prepared baking dish, covering with the dough strips in a lattice design.
10. Bake at 425°F for 15 minutes.
11. Reduce temperature to 350°F and bake an additional 30 minutes.
12. Serve warm. Yield: 10 servings.
Formatted by Elaine Radis
